macro so uh today's key macro question
is what do the latest hard data signal
about the outlook for the US economy our
answers are the November retail sales
data were highly supportive of our
resilient US economy theme the November
industrial production data were
unsupportive of our resilient US economy
think recall that our empirical Deep
dive study of us business cycle confirms
that these time series are persistent
leading indicators of the business cycle
which means they lead on the way up and
on the way down the latest retail sales
and Auto Sales data suggest that no
Landing is the most likely US economic
outcome over the medium term the latest
industrial production and capacity
utilization data suggest a soft Landing
is the most likely US economic outcome
over the medium term given the relative
of consuming things versus producing
things in the US economy we believe this
Divergence Nets out in favor of the no
Landing scenario being the most probable
our trip s's theme will have something
to say about that as we learn more about
the size sequence and scope of President
Trump's policy implementation over the
medium term transition tr42 micro
dashboard is here as always we wrap up
with a question from our client this
one's regarding TGA spindown says hi

perform much differently during the 2021
TJ spin down than they did during the

feature you have mentioned uh for q1 I
was wondering if you thought it
worthwhile to dig into the Nuance of the
differences or if you think that would
be wasted effort so certainly not wasted

take it to the chart to uh kind of
explain you know the key takeaways of
what I'm trying to uh uh convey so uh
back at so the the key the real the
conclusion is the 2022 TGA spin down
coincided with a significant
acceleration significant increase in the
fed's reverse supero facility balance
which ultimately caused net liquidity
decline you can see this here uh TJ spin
down here in 2022 significant increase
in uh the RP balance and the net result
of that was that we had actually
declining us liquidity uh throughout the
um throughout most of 2022 the the
periods that we're highlighting in terms
of uh the comparable periods we're
highlighting with respect to our uh
bullish forecast for the TJ spin down in
q1 of 2025 coincide with the dead
selling impass that we achieved back in
2021 and the dead selling impass that we
uh uh hit uh back in uh 20123 um we saw
$85 billion of TJ spend down during the
2021 Deb s imp pass we saw roughly $560
billion of TGA spend down during the
2023 uh uh uh the T deing impass and
obviously both of those uh contributed
to Raging Bull markets in Risk assets so
it's our belief that you're probably not
going to see a significant increase in
the RRP in q1 of next year during the
likely TJ spend down uh in that time
frame assuming they don't punt on the
dead selling moratorium uh uh uh
deadline uh if they if they maintain the
January 1 deadline then we're probably
going to see the TGA start to get spent
down as the treasury will be without
borrowing Authority until they legislate
a change uh and and recall that you need
60 votes in the Senate to uh enact a
incre debt selling increase and so uh
you know it's very unlikely the
Democrats are going to just you know go
go quietly into the night uh and just
allow you know the Republican party uh
to get what they want they go to they
got to use the debt celling uh to
negotiate uh you know whatever their
policy priorities are in the upcoming
reconciliation budget so investors need
to be aware of that and so going back to
the RP recall that we had a uh you know
the Fed was psyching interest rates is
the fastest Pace in 40 years uh in the
RP so what happened was the uh investors
were fleeing money out of duration
sensitive assets both in the stock and
bond markets primar in the bond market
and putting that money in Money Market
funds we also saw a significant uh
outflow of capital from uh Bank deposits
as well into money market funds uh in
that time period as well and so in our
opinion in the context of the FED having
this asymmetrically doish reaction
function at the current juncture and
likely continuing to lower the policy
rate at the bare minimum just got to a
much lower neutral rate than they're
currently at today in our opinion we
don't think there's a significant risk
of a dramatic increase in the RP balance
in q1 to offset what is is likely to be
